column1: # MathSolver evaluator reference corpus — `mathsolver eval` end-to-end cases.
# Same format and matching semantics as cases.tsv; see README.md in this
# Reference values were computed with mpmath at 50 significant digits from the
# EXACT rational arguments (MathSolver parses decimal literals as exact
# then independently cross-checked against double-precision math-module
# computations (agreement required to 1e-12 relative, far tighter than the
# The python used is reproduced in the comment block at the bottom.
# Ids start at ev-20 / err-20 so they stay unique even if merged with cases.tsv.
# near-but-not-at domain edge: asin argument is exactly 999999/1000000
# tanh(20) = 1 - 4.1e-18; indistinguishable from 1 at double precision
# near-edge of the ln domain; no scientific-notation literals (section 12),
# so 1e-9 is spelled out and parses as the exact rational 1/1000000000
# plain log is base 10; 0.001 parses as exactly 1/1000
# negative base with INTEGER exponent is fine
# negative base, odd-denominator rational exponent: exact factory fold (section 2)
# exact result 2^101 exists but overflows 64 bits, so the Pow stays symbolic
# (section 2) and the evaluator computes it in doubles: 2^101 = 2.5353...e30
# 0.1 in a binding is the double nearest 1/10; expected computed from exact
# (1/10)^2 - 2*(1/10) + 1 = 81/100 (the two agree to ~1e-16, well under tol)
# negative base is legal because the evaluated exponent is the integer 3
# folds to EXACTLY 0 in the factories (1/10 + 2/10 - 3/10), unlike doubles
# ---------- overflow-adjacent exact arithmetic that must still evaluate ----------
# Mul(2^62, Pow(2^60, -1)) must fold exactly (128-bit intermediates, section 2)
# ---------- error cases (section 6; diagnostics on stderr, exit 1) ----------
# negative base, even-denominator exponent: never folds, EvalError at eval time
# mathematically real (cube root of -5) but 5 is not a perfect cube so the Pow
# cannot fold exactly; section 6 pins non-integer exponent + negative base -> EvalError
# cosh(1000) overflows the double range -> non-finite result -> EvalError
# unbound-symbol message must NAME the symbol (section 6)
# x is bound; the message must name the unbound q
# Each value was cross-checked against an independent double-precision
# math-module computation; asserts require agreement to 1e-12 relative.
#   from fractions import Fraction as F
#   def R(num, den=1):            # exact rational -> 50-digit mpf
#   for cid, hi, lo in cases:
#   # exact-rational sanity checks backing ev-60/61/65/66/67/68 and ev-26:
# Exact-value cases not in the list above: ev-38/39 (|-7|=7, |2-5|=3),
# by the section-2 odd-denominator fold), ev-62 ((-2)^3=-8 via bindings),
# ev-65 (exactly 0), ev-68 (exactly 4) — all pure rational arithmetic.
